{"ProviderType":"GitHub","CommitMessage":"feat: add AWS-native CI/CD pipeline with InspectorScan security gates

Phase 3 complete. Full pipeline: Source (GitHub) -> SAST scan -> Build ->
Image scan -> Deploy to ECS. All AWS-native, all in Terraform.

Pipeline (CodePipeline V2, required for InspectorScan actions):
- Source: GitHub via CodeConnection (OAuth completed in console)
- SASTScan: InspectorScan SourceCodeScan action
- Build: CodeBuild builds image, pushes SHA-tagged to ECR (immutable)
- ImageScan: InspectorScan ECRImageScan, CALIBRATED to baseline (2C/7H/8M):
  normal image passes, regressions above baseline are blocked
- Deploy: ECS rolling deploy via imagedefinitions.json

Least-privilege service roles: separate scoped roles for CodeBuild and
CodePipeline, distinct from the human build user.

IAM refactor: hit the 10-managed-policy-per-user limit mid-phase. Consolidated
ten incremental phase policies into three function-scoped policies
(infra-core, containers, pipeline), each under the 6 KB cap. Incremental
originals retained in-repo as a record of the least-privilege discovery.

What broke and what I learned:
- InspectorScan actions require pipeline_type V2; Terraform defaults to V1
- Enhanced ECR scanning is powered by Inspector v2 -- needs its own
  service-linked role, a third SLR beyond ELB and ECS
- Threshold philosophy: calibrated gates to baseline beat naive zero-tolerance
  gates that teams end up disabling

Known limitations: threshold gating is count-based (co"}

Image scan -> Deploy to ECS. All AWS-native, all in Terraform.

Pipeline (CodePipeline V2, required for InspectorScan actions):
- Source: GitHub via CodeConnection (OAuth completed in console)
- SASTScan: InspectorScan SourceCodeScan action
- Build: CodeBuild builds image, pushes SHA-tagged to ECR (immutable)
- ImageScan: InspectorScan ECRImageScan, CALIBRATED to baseline (2C/7H/8M):
  normal image passes, regressions above baseline are blocked
- Deploy: ECS rolling deploy via imagedefinitions.json

Least-privilege service roles: separate scoped roles for CodeBuild and
CodePipeline, distinct from the human build user.

IAM refactor: hit the 10-managed-policy-per-user limit mid-phase. Consolidated
ten incremental phase policies into three function-scoped policies
(infra-core, containers, pipeline), each under the 6 KB cap. Incremental
originals retained in-repo as a record of the least-privilege discovery.

What broke and what I learned:
- InspectorScan actions require pipeline_type V2; Terraform defaults to V1
- Enhanced ECR scanning is powered by Inspector v2 -- needs its own
  service-linked role, a third SLR beyond ELB and ECS
- Threshold philosophy: calibrated gates to baseline beat naive zero-tolerance
  gates that teams end up disabling

Known limitations: threshold gating is count-based (could miss a vuln at/under

{"ProviderType":"GitHub","CommitMessage":"feat: provision VPC, ALB, ECS Fargate, and least-privilege IAM roles

Phase 2 complete. App runs on Fargate behind an ALB, reachable only through
the load balancer. All infrastructure defined in Terraform.

Architecture:
- VPC with 2 public subnets across AZs (no NAT: ~$32/mo saved; tradeoff noted)
- Paired security groups: ALB allows :80 from internet, task SG allows :8080
  ONLY from the ALB SG -- compensating control for public-subnet tasks
- ECS Fargate, 1 task @ 0.25vCPU/512MB, image pulled from ECR (v1)
- Separate task execution role (ECR pull + logs) and task role (empty by
  design; the app makes no AWS API calls)
- CloudWatch log group wired into the task definition

What broke and what I learned:
- 5 IAM permission walls. Discovered least privilege incrementally rather
  than granting ec2:* / elasticloadbalancing:*
- VPC module modifies AWS's DEFAULT NACL and security group to harden them,
  requiring NACL-entry and SG-rule actions beyond simple create perms
- ALB and ECS each require a one-time service-linked role. Scoped both grants
  to the exact role ARN plus an iam:AWSServiceName condition key
- Terraform caught an AWS constraint: security group descriptions reject '>'
- ELB read-back actions (DescribeListenerAttributes) only surface at apply time

Known limitations (production would differ):
- HTTP only; prod terminates HTTPS on the ALB with an ACM cert
- 1 task, no HA; prod runs >=2 across AZs
- Tasks in public subnets; pro"}

outputVariables.CommitMessage:
feat: provision VPC, ALB, ECS Fargate, and least-privilege IAM roles

Phase 2 complete. App runs on Fargate behind an ALB, reachable only through
the load balancer. All infrastructure defined in Terraform.

Architecture:
- VPC with 2 public subnets across AZs (no NAT: ~$32/mo saved; tradeoff noted)
- Paired security groups: ALB allows :80 from internet, task SG allows :8080
  ONLY from the ALB SG -- compensating control for public-subnet tasks
- ECS Fargate, 1 task @ 0.25vCPU/512MB, image pulled from ECR (v1)
- Separate task execution role (ECR pull + logs) and task role (empty by
  design; the app makes no AWS API calls)
- CloudWatch log group wired into the task definition

What broke and what I learned:
- 5 IAM permission walls. Discovered least privilege incrementally rather
  than granting ec2:* / elasticloadbalancing:*
- VPC module modifies AWS's DEFAULT NACL and security group to harden them,
  requiring NACL-entry and SG-rule actions beyond simple create perms
- ALB and ECS each require a one-time service-linked role. Scoped both grants
  to the exact role ARN plus an iam:AWSServiceName condition key
- Terraform caught an AWS constraint: security group descriptions reject '>'
- ELB read-back actions (DescribeListenerAttributes) only surface at apply time

Known limitations (production would differ):
- HTTP only; prod terminates HTTPS on the ALB with an ACM cert
- 1 task, no HA; prod runs >=2 across AZs
- Tasks in public subnets; prod uses private subnets + NAT or VPC endpoints
- Local Terraform state; p
